context: The university rolls out 'Mentor Mirror,' a glowing, high-tech AI dashboard available to all faculty. It algorithmically tracks student 'cognitive output,' engagement metrics, and predicts failure rates. A human professor has explicitly chosen to override and ignore this dashboard for a struggling student, arguing that the AI only measures standardized output, while the professor needs to measure the student's 'intent' and guide them through productive struggle.
friction_point: Efficiency vs. Humanistic Pedagogy. The tension between scalable algorithmic profiling (which optimizes for retention metrics) and unscalable human mentorship (which allows for the 'Death of Becoming' and formative failure).
